title: Forgiveness Is Mine to Choose, on My Time
subtitle: A three-minute affirmation for the woman who has been told it is time to forgive
excerpt: A three-minute affirmation for the woman who has been told it is time to forgive: by family, by faith, by self-help. The affirmation does not refuse forgiveness. It refuses the timetable.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The forgiveness-on-my-time shape is a staple of writing on forgiveness and on self-trust.

content.text: A three-minute affirmation for the woman who has been told it is time to forgive. Said three times slow, hand on the heart.
New to affirmation as a practice? Read

There is a lot of pressure around forgiveness. Faith communities push for it. Self-help books push for it. Family members push for it. The reason is usually that the people around you would feel easier if you did.
The affirmation does not refuse forgiveness. It refuses the timetable. Forgiveness, when it comes, is yours.
Forgiveness is mine to choose. I get to choose it on my time. The timetable is mine, not theirs.
Sit somewhere quiet. Hand on the heart. Say the affirmation three times, slow.
Use it before a family event, after a conversation where you have been pressured, or any time the timetable-talk turns up.
Original to homemade.education. The on-my-time shape is a common one. It appears in writing on forgiveness and on self-trust.
